title: Webdis Configuration
description: Schema describing the supported configuration options for the Rust Webdis server.

$schema.description: Path or URL to this schema file so editors can enable validation.

redis_host.description: Hostname or IP address of the target Redis server.

redis_port.description: TCP port of the target Redis server.

redis_socket.description: Filesystem path to a Redis UNIX-domain socket. When set, Webdis prefers this over redis_host/redis_port. TLS (ssl) is not applicable to UNIX sockets.

redis_auth.description: Authentication parameters passed to Redis. Use a password string for Redis < 6 or an array of [username, password] for ACL-based auth.

http_host.description: Interface Webdis binds to for HTTP traffic.

http_threads.description: HTTP concurrency setting used for Redis pool sizing and other HTTP-side capacity decisions.

runtime_worker_threads.description: Override for the Tokio runtime worker-thread count. When omitted, Tokio chooses its default worker count.

threads.description: Legacy alias for http_threads. Prefer http_threads; when both exist, http_threads takes precedence.

pool_size_per_thread.description: Number of Redis connections allocated per HTTP worker thread.

pool_size.description: Legacy alias for pool_size_per_thread. Prefer pool_size_per_thread; when both exist, pool_size_per_thread takes precedence.

database.description: Redis logical database index selected after connecting.

websockets.description: Enable WebSocket endpoint (/.json) for Pub/Sub and command execution.

acl.description: Ordered list of ACL rules evaluated from top to bottom.

http_max_request_size.description: Maximum accepted HTTP request size in bytes.

default_root.description: Redis command executed when the root path (/) is requested, e.g. /GET/index.html.

hiredis.description: Legacy Hiredis keep-alive settings kept for compatibility. When keep_alive_sec is set, Webdis configures TCP keep-alive on Redis TCP/TLS connections. This does not apply to UNIX sockets (redis_socket).

keep_alive_sec.description: Keep-alive idle time (seconds) used for Redis TCP/TLS connections. The probe interval is derived as approximately keep_alive_sec / 3 when supported by the platform.

aclRule.description: ACL rule that can allow or deny commands based on HTTP auth and/or IP address.

disabled.description: Redis commands disabled for clients matching this rule.

enabled.description: Redis commands enabled despite previous disabled matches.

http_basic_auth.description: Value for HTTP Basic Auth in the form username:password.

ip.description: IP address or CIDR range matched against the client.

sslConfig.description: Configuration for TLS connections to Redis.

enabled.description: Set to true to enable TLS when connecting to Redis.

ca_cert_bundle.description: Path to the CA certificate bundle used to validate Redis.

path_to_certs.description: Optional directory containing trusted CA certificates.

redis_sni.description: Override SNI hostname when negotiating TLS with Redis.
